questions.text: The line segment joining a vertex of a triangle to the mid-point of its opposite side is called a:
questions.explanation: A median connects a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side. A triangle has three medians.

questions.text: The perpendicular line segment from a vertex of a triangle to its opposite side is called an:
questions.explanation: The altitude represents the height of the triangle from a specific vertex.

questions.text: An exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of its two interior ________ angles.
questions.explanation: Exterior Angle Property: An exterior angle equals the sum of its interior opposite angles.

questions.text: The total measure of the three angles of a triangle is:
questions.explanation: Angle Sum Property: The sum of all interior angles of any triangle is always 180°.

questions.text: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite to the right angle is called the:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is the longest side in a right-angled triangle.

questions.text: A triangle in which all three sides are of equal length is called:
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, each angle also measures 60°.

questions.text: In an isosceles triangle, if the non-equal angle is 40°, what is the measure of each equal angle?
Which of the following can be the sides of a triangle?
questions.explanation: Triangle Inequality: Sum of any two sides must be greater than the third side. 6+8 > 10.

questions.text: The Pythagoras property states that in a right-angled triangle:
questions.explanation: The square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides.

questions.text: Find the value of x if the angles of a triangle are x, 2x, and 3x.
How many altitudes does a triangle have?
questions.explanation: Every triangle has exactly three altitudes, one from each vertex.

questions.text: In an equilateral triangle, each angle measures:
If two sides of a triangle are 4 cm and 7 cm, the third side must be greater than:
questions.explanation: The difference between two sides (7 - 4 = 3) must be less than the third side.

questions.text: A triangle cannot have more than one:
questions.explanation: If a triangle had two right angles, the sum would be 180° before the third angle.

questions.text: In a triangle, an exterior angle is 110° and one interior opposite angle is 30°. The other interior opposite angle is:
The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side.
questions.explanation: This is the fundamental triangle inequality property.

questions.text: If the sides of a triangle are 3 cm, 4 cm, and 5 cm, it is a ________ triangle.
questions.explanation: 3² + 4² = 9 + 16 = 25 = 5². It satisfies Pythagoras property.

questions.text: Which of the following can be the angles of a triangle?
In an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite to equal sides are:
questions.explanation: Base angles of an isosceles triangle are always equal.

questions.text: If an exterior angle of a triangle is 100° and its interior opposite angles are equal, each is:
The longest side of a right-angled triangle is the:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is always the longest side because it is opposite the largest angle (90°).

questions.text: Is it possible to have a triangle with sides 10.2 cm, 5.8 cm and 4.5 cm?
questions.explanation: 5.8 + 4.5 = 10.3, which is greater than 10.2. Yes, it is possible.

questions.text: Find the hypotenuse of a right triangle whose legs are 6 cm and 8 cm.
In triangle ABC, if ∠A = 90°, then BC² is equal to:
questions.explanation: Pythagoras theorem: Hypotenuse² = Base² + Altitude².

questions.text: An obtuse-angled triangle has ________ obtuse angle(s).
questions.explanation: A triangle can only have one obtuse angle (greater than 90°).

questions.text: Which property relates the sides of a right-angled triangle?
questions.explanation: Pythagoras property is specific to right-angled triangles.

questions.text: The difference of lengths of any two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side.
questions.explanation: In any triangle, the difference of any two sides is always less than the third side.

questions.text: If a triangle has two angles of 60° each, it must be:
questions.explanation: The third angle must be 180 - (60+60) = 60°. All angles are 60°, making it equilateral.

questions.text: A ladder 15 m long reaches a window 12 m high. How far is the foot of the ladder from the wall?
The point where the three medians of a triangle meet is called the:
questions.explanation: The centroid is the balance point of the triangle.

questions.text: In a right-angled triangle, if one acute angle is 35°, the other acute angle is:
Which of the following is NOT a property of triangles?
options: Sum of two sides > third side
Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles
questions.explanation: A triangle can have at most one right angle.

questions.text: If the sides of a triangle are equal, then its angles are:
questions.explanation: This is a property of equilateral triangles.

questions.text: A triangle whose two angles are 45° and 45° is a ________ triangle.
questions.explanation: Third angle = 180 - 90 = 90°. Two angles are equal, so it's isosceles.

questions.text: Which vertex is opposite to side BC in triangle ABC?
questions.explanation: Vertex A is the point not on the side BC.

questions.text: The perimeter of a triangle with sides 5 cm, 7 cm, and 9 cm is:
In an isosceles right-angled triangle, the acute angles are:
questions.explanation: One angle is 90°, the other two must be equal and sum to 90°.

questions.text: The altitude of a triangle always lies in the interior of the triangle.
questions.explanation: In obtuse triangles, altitudes can fall outside the triangle.

questions.text: If the square of the longest side of a triangle is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides, the triangle is:
questions.explanation: This is the converse of the Pythagoras theorem.

questions.text: Median of a triangle ________ the triangle into two equal area triangles.
questions.explanation: A median divides a triangle into two triangles of equal area.
